creator: ripley@nostromo.in-berlin.de
text:
Booted the 3.2-RELEASE floppies (stupid bios fails to
	recognize SCSI-CDROM as bootable), started installation
	and selected "CDROM" as install source.  Popup says it 
	failed to find a CD-ROM drive.  Running the emergency
	shell doesn't help as no appropriate mount cmd is on
	/stand.  During kernel initialization the drive has
	been probed correctly.

Fix: 

Check for /dev/acd0c _and_ /dev/cd0c.
How-To-Repeat: 
	Attempt to install on said (or similar) hardware
